reviews_data.review_text: This used to be our favorite bakery in all of Charlotte when we first moved here. Since then it has gotten more popular which is great but the quality has gone down tremendously. We’ve started to explore other bakeries instead but as a former favorite we decided to come back and see if the quality had gotten any better. Ordered a ham and cheese croissant only to find a hair in it. They offered to replace it in lieu of a refund only for the second one to be extremely cold in the middle. I decided on just leaving it as it really dampened the experience. I do hope the quality gets better as it used to be but unfortunately I won’t be returning.
The croissant was fantastic.

reviews_data.review_text:
A Top Flight Experience

When you decide to dine out, and select an expensive experience you want to have your choice validated.
Nobody wants to drop a few hundred dollars, spend an hour or more, and be disappointed.
COPAIN (Koh-pahn), from top to bottom delivered.
Service? Charming, knowledgeable and just the right amount of attention.
Food? Duck breast and chicken with wine reduction (Canard L’ Orange, coq au vin) were excellent.
Dessert? Is it art, or food? Turns out, it’s both. Delicious.
The ambience was very good, with acoustics that were well thought out removing the loud, reverberating noise that so many restaurants unfortunately feature.
Highly recommend.

Beautiful cafe with a nice selection of pastries and pleasant service. I've recently tasted a raspberry donut, an almond croissant, a whipped cream puff, and some samples. They were not the freshest to admire perfection; but all were quite decent, sweet treats. I want to address a rye bread. I've lived in this area for 8 years and visited it before the store's new design. I was buying rye bread more often. I've admired perfection and exceptional taste that could only be experienced in Europe. A lot of time has passed since then, and I've learnt to bake bread myself so I can enjoy European-style bread daily. I visited the cafe yesterday and bought $11 worth of rye sourdough bread. With a heavy heart, I am noticing a significant difference in taste and quality. I won't be buying bread from Copain anymore. Unsurpassed perfection is gone; it's just regular bread with poor taste and dough quality, easy to notice for a customer with a European palate.
Unique and beautiful atmosphere, good amount of seating, great bread and pastries at pretty good prices. Definitely recommend the pecan twist.

reviews_data.review_text: The new Copain in Ballantyne is fantastic!
The coffee was excellent, and I enjoyed my visit. I would definitely recommend it to others. We ordered the peppermint and mocha, both were delicious.

Very nice little French boutique type of café. Not too many coffee choices, but enough. Lots of baked goods. Cozy atmosphere with plenty of seating, snd prices are within the norm. Lots of good varieties besides baked goods.

My husband has recently been talking about home country France and I wanted to bring him to a nice French bakery. When we first got there the staff didn’t treat me and my husband like everyone else, I assumed that it was because I am 2% African American and I’m not French. Though I decided not to pay attention even though the staff clearly were giving me attitude and when I asked for a croissant they said they didn’t have any even though it was clearly served to their other customers. My husband who ordered macarons was fully enraged because there was hair in his macaroon I even have a picture of it saved. He was so enraged that his epilepsy was triggered, he suffered a bad seizure and the staff didn’t do a thing in fact they looked and just did absolutely nothing honestly this was horrible I called 911 and he was rushed to the hospital immediately when I confronted the staff they denied all accountability and didn’t admit there was hair in the macaron. This was a traumatic experience for me and I definitely didn’t expect people to be so apathetic. I had a horrible experience the food wasn’t good felt underbaked and the service was horrible. Im giving 1 star because I can’t give 0 but if i could I would. I just wanted to share my experience if you think about coming here.
Cute bakery with lots of room to sit and socialize. They do seem to get orders wrong at times so I would double check what you get.
Great place, cozy atmosphere at Ballantyne location. We need more places like this in Charlotte 🥰

Delicious food & incredible coffee, but awful service. We waited 20+ minutes for our coffees & parfait. When we inquired we were told they were coming. We waited an additional 5-10 minutes to inquire again. They then began making them (suspecting they just forgot)  and handed us the parfait without an apology. Kind of cold / unfriendly. It’s a lovely atmosphere but given there are many cute, French inspired cafes in this area I’d opt for a different one!
SO GOOD

Paris 🇫🇷 in Charlotte
My son had previously visited Copain Bakery while shopping with one of his university friends, so when we found ourselves nearby close to closing time, we stopped in. As a high-level Google Guide, I often reach out to establishments ahead of time, and I introduced myself to Laure (pronounced Lor-ee), who is from France and manages the bakery. She does a wonderful job—we even took a sweet photo together.
I tasted the cappuccino my son ordered, and we absolutely devoured the most delicious chocolate orange tart. I also took home a loaf of Pain au Levain, which stayed wonderful for days.
I’m already planning to return to try some lunch options, along with more desserts and breads—and then a plan to try Rooster’s nearby.
I am happiest exploring and tasting life, and as a globally-published cancer researcher, I can tell you: life is far too short not to indulge from time to time.

Josephine Local 🍴

The food was great. We did have two issues while we were there, though: the bacon & cheese biscuit was internally cool and the latte took forever to come out. I wasn’t the only one complaining about the food being cool. Another customer was also complaining about the same thing with his food and the manager had the audacity to tell us “it’s because the temperature outside is too cold” like we’re children. Honestly, the customer service was subpar. The sitting area was nice.

The croissants are probably the best in Charlotte, NC! The quiches are great, too~

Review update, as I come here weekly: as they've expanded, they now have more pastry options, which I appreciate. However, the croissants are now more performative - prettier but drier & less flake, less airy. The individual quiche slices are also a bit smaller. They also used to be served with a mini side salad, not anymore.
Variety of pastries to choose from. Friendly service.

We travel to Copain Bakery & Boulangerie for their Phenomenal Breads & Pastries from Gastonia weekly. It is truly worth the drive. Their Almond Croissants, Sourdough Breads, Baguettes, Gingerbread Cookies, and their Strawberry Macha Crème Brûlé are WOW, simply A-MAZ-ING!! They bring me back to a French Boulangerie! Their Coffee is marvelous. This is a Bakery in Charlotte in the true French style that is a MAJOR impact for Charlotte. You MUST go to experience this delightful and spectacular staff, pastries, & breads when in Charlotte. Venturing from Gastonia to Charlotte anytime we get the chance makes our final destination here to Copain so wonderful! Please get some delicious and artistic breads & pastries from Copain!!!

What a cute spot! I love French bakeries and I was excited to win a friend recommended this place in Charlotte! I met her here for brunch and had an egg sandwich along with a French bake, which was delicious. I wish I had taken some desserts home with me as there were so many that were calling my name. Friendly staff members, and just a cute and quaint place overall. There are a few tables within the cafe and several right outside in the common area. Can't wait for another opportunity to go back!

The almond croissants are heavenly! In December, experience the gingerbread men & gingerbread macaroons so decadent & delightful! We come into Charlotte from Gastonia & it is extremely worth the drive! We truly enjoy the Pain Au Levain to take home to enjoy Copain for the remainder of the week! Copain is a spectacular bakery, boulangerie with its beautiful cafe! This is a delightful place! Come check it out and take some bread to go!

Incredible pastries. They are doing baking right. It is clear there is a lot of effort and expertise put into each dish. The cherry cheesecake danish was absolutely perfectly flaky and soft, not too sweet. The crust of the spinach feta quiche was top notch. Highly recommend.

reviews_data.review_text:
The whole visit was a HUGE LETDOWN, there was NO charm at all.

My croissant was dry, oddly dense, and tasted like it had been sitting out since the day before.
My daughter's chocolate chip cookie was burnt.
The sourdough was wet & raw in the middle....you could see it was under baked.
The staff was not able to slice the bread for this reason.

For the prices they charge, I expected much higher quality and at least a little warmth.

The atmosphere was cold--both literally and figuratively.

No warmth, no charm, and there was definitely NO welcoming vibe.
The whole experience felt rushed and impersonal.

If you're looking for warmth, and French charm this bakery is absolutely not the place.
The décor is sterile, and the vibe is lifeless.
NOT a cozy bakery experience.

Overall, definitely not worth the hype.
There are plenty of other bakeries that care about both their food and atmosphere--sadly, this is NOT one of them.

reviews_data.review_text: The wonderful Copain has become our place of rest and relaxation in Charlotte. I grew up in the Northeast where my mom regularly stopped by local artisan bakeries, and I just simply got spoiled on Great Bread.  Well, Copain is the first bakery that I have found that makes amazing artisan bread that is crispy/crunchy on the outside and soft/chewy on the inside.  The density is awesome.  A good loaf of bread should be heavy, not light as air.  I can't stand cheap grocery store bread that looks like it should and disappoints.   Try Copain.   You won't be disappointed.   The other pastries, salads, and other goods are excellent too.

reviews_data.review_text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
A Little Piece of Paris in Charlotte!

Copain Bakery & Provisions completely blew me away! I stopped in for a sweet treat and ended up having one of the best pastry experiences I’ve had outside of Europe. The cream puff was airy and delicate, with just the right amount of vanilla filling—light, not overly sweet, and perfectly balanced.

The éclair was next-level. Rich, smooth chocolate on top and a silky custard filling that made every bite indulgent without being too heavy. You can tell it’s made with real, quality ingredients and French technique.

And the macarons? Perfection. Crisp outer shells with chewy centers and flavorful fillings that actually taste like what they’re supposed to—my favorites was the coffee flavor.

The staff was warm and welcoming, and the space itself is elegant yet cozy. Whether you’re grabbing a quick treat or sitting down to enjoy a coffee and pastry, Copain is an absolute must for any bakery lover.

I’ll definitely be back when in Charlotte!

reviews_data.review_text: You may want to get here early if you would like the best chance at getting a table. I visited mid-morning on a weekday and was surprised it was so busy. Although it’s a nice place to meet for groups (classes, games, needlepoint), those guests take away the space from patrons with smaller parties who would flip tables more quickly…especially when the majority of seating is reserved for Roosters. Both the vegetarian croissant breakfast sandwich and ham and egg croissant were delicious, and the Boston Crème pastry was to die for. I wish the customer service and attitude of the cashiers matched the level of superiority of their dishes. It was rather overwhelming to walk in as a first timer, not know the menu, and not have an employee offer to assist in any way or answer questions (in fact, they seemed annoyed that I didn’t order quickly enough, which made me feel even more pressured to finish the ordeal). The line to order is confusing and I don’t believe I was the only one who thought so given the number of times I was asked if I was in line by other customers. This is partially due to onlookers and shoppers browsing the many gift items on display. If I go back, I will take things to go instead of the hassle of crowded tables, overwhelm, and attitude. My party and I did purchase a half loaf of bread to go which was fresh and lovely. Overall, an outstanding product, but their interactions leave much to be desired. If you’d like to feel like you’re in France and don’t mind being treated as less than equal, this is a great place for you.

I am not saying this to be rude to his opinion but I don’t respect his conclusion and how he handled it. This place takes coffee to a new level.

I am a coffee drinker, who usually finds it rather noneventful.  I have my daily cup at home, or a cup or two on the weekend with my wife.  It has always been a social thing for me.  I drink about 1/3 of the cup, and leave the rest.

But not at Copain Boulangerie, oh no, I sit back and relax, sip on a drip, americano, or perhaps a smooth cappuccino, mindfully enjoying my surroundings.

I have worked in Southpark for 11 years, and just recently discovered Copain.  They are less than five minutes away, and I never knew they were there.

This quaint little French bakery and coffee shop, is warm and inviting, and if only for the moment, takes me away from the outside world.

My first experience was with Emily. She poured me a warm cup of the most flavorful drip coffee that I had ever tasted!

I finished my large cup of delicious brew, and as I left with my second cup to go, I turned to Emily, and asked her if she put sugar in my coffee.  She told me, matter-of-factly, that she had not.

I thanked her, told her that I would be back soon, and that I never want sugar in my coffee at Copain.

And don’t get me started on their baked goods.  The coconut tart was heavenly, and the ham and egg scone was delicious.

Alison, another worker, introduced me to the world of croissants.  I have had croissants before, but have never really cared for them. I enjoyed this one!

Amy, the Manager at Copain, told me about how Copain began, what they are about, their offerings, and their plans to be open on Sundays, starting the last week in July.

Sunday is the only day of the week, that I get to spend the whole day with my wife, so I am so very excited about being able to share this experience with her.

reviews_data.review_text:
A Top Flight Experience

When you decide to dine out, and select an expensive experience you want to have your choice validated.
Nobody wants to drop a few hundred dollars, spend an hour or more, and be disappointed.
COPAIN (Koh-pahn), from top to bottom delivered.
Service? Charming, knowledgeable and just the right amount of attention.
Food? Duck breast and chicken with wine reduction (Canard L’ Orange, coq au vin) were excellent.
Dessert? Is it art, or food? Turns out, it’s both. Delicious.
The ambience was very good, with acoustics that were well thought out removing the loud, reverberating noise that so many restaurants unfortunately feature.
Highly recommend.

Beautiful cafe with a nice selection of pastries and pleasant service. I've recently tasted a raspberry donut, an almond croissant, a whipped cream puff, and some samples. They were not the freshest to admire perfection; but all were quite decent, sweet treats. I want to address a rye bread. I've lived in this area for 8 years and visited it before the store's new design. I was buying rye bread more often. I've admired perfection and exceptional taste that could only be experienced in Europe. A lot of time has passed since then, and I've learnt to bake bread myself so I can enjoy European-style bread daily. I visited the cafe yesterday and bought $11 worth of rye sourdough bread. With a heavy heart, I am noticing a significant difference in taste and quality. I won't be buying bread from Copain anymore. Unsurpassed perfection is gone; it's just regular bread with poor taste and dough quality, easy to notice for a customer with a European palate.
Unique and beautiful atmosphere, good amount of seating, great bread and pastries at pretty good prices. Definitely recommend the pecan twist.

When we first got there the staff didn’t treat me and my husband like everyone else, I assumed that it was because I am 2% African American and I’m not French. Though I decided not to pay attention even though the staff clearly were giving me attitude and when I asked for a croissant they said they didn’t have any even though it was clearly served to their other customers. My husband who ordered macarons was fully enraged because there was hair in his macaroon I even have a picture of it saved. He was so enraged that his epilepsy was triggered, he suffered a bad seizure and the staff didn’t do a thing in fact they looked and just did absolutely nothing honestly this was horrible I called 911 and he was rushed to the hospital immediately when I confronted the staff they denied all accountability and didn’t admit there was hair in the macaron. This was a traumatic experience for me and I definitely didn’t expect people to be so apathetic. I had a horrible experience the food wasn’t good felt underbaked and the service was horrible. Im giving 1 star because I can’t give 0 but if i could I would. I just wanted to share my experience if you think about coming here.

A Little Piece of Paris in Charlotte!

Copain Bakery & Provisions completely blew me away! I stopped in for a sweet treat and ended up having one of the best pastry experiences I’ve had outside of Europe. The cream puff was airy and delicate, with just the right amount of vanilla filling—light, not overly sweet, and perfectly balanced.

The éclair was next-level. Rich, smooth chocolate on top and a silky custard filling that made every bite indulgent without being too heavy. You can tell it’s made with real, quality ingredients and French technique.

And the macarons? Perfection. Crisp outer shells with chewy centers and flavorful fillings that actually taste like what they’re supposed to—my favorites was the coffee flavor.

The staff was warm and welcoming, and the space itself is elegant yet cozy. Whether you’re grabbing a quick treat or sitting down to enjoy a coffee and pastry, Copain is an absolute must for any bakery lover.

I’ll definitely be back when in Charlotte!

reviews_data.review_text: You may want to get here early if you would like the best chance at getting a table. I visited mid-morning on a weekday and was surprised it was so busy. Although it’s a nice place to meet for groups (classes, games, needlepoint), those guests take away the space from patrons with smaller parties who would flip tables more quickly…especially when the majority of seating is reserved for Roosters. Both the vegetarian croissant breakfast sandwich and ham and egg croissant were delicious, and the Boston Crème pastry was to die for. I wish the customer service and attitude of the cashiers matched the level of superiority of their dishes. It was rather overwhelming to walk in as a first timer, not know the menu, and not have an employee offer to assist in any way or answer questions (in fact, they seemed annoyed that I didn’t order quickly enough, which made me feel even more pressured to finish the ordeal). The line to order is confusing and I don’t believe I was the only one who thought so given the number of times I was asked if I was in line by other customers. This is partially due to onlookers and shoppers browsing the many gift items on display. If I go back, I will take things to go instead of the hassle of crowded tables, overwhelm, and attitude. My party and I did purchase a half loaf of bread to go which was fresh and lovely. Overall, an outstanding product, but their interactions leave much to be desired. If you’d like to feel like you’re in France and don’t mind being treated as less than equal, this is a great place for you.

I am not one that normally writes a review but I learned of this from word of mouth and was curious and went to google reviews while sitting here and the first review I saw was someone that said they were not impressed. Instead of kindly just explaining to the establishment he was not satisfied and giving them a chance to make it right because he doesn’t like confrontation instead he got behind his keyboard to say negative things.

I saw where Copain responded and they of course would have gladly remade the drink because if anything this could have been a teaching moment for one of the baristas that may have made it wrong. reviews_data.review_text:
This place takes coffee to a new level.

I am a coffee drinker, who usually finds it rather noneventful.  I have my daily cup at home, or a cup or two on the weekend with my wife.  It has always been a social thing for me.  I drink about 1/3 of the cup, and leave the rest.

But not at Copain Boulangerie, oh no, I sit back and relax, sip on a drip, americano, or perhaps a smooth cappuccino, mindfully enjoying my surroundings.

I have worked in Southpark for 11 years, and just recently discovered Copain.  They are less than five minutes away, and I never knew they were there.

This quaint little French bakery and coffee shop, is warm and inviting, and if only for the moment, takes me away from the outside world.

My first experience was with Emily. She poured me a warm cup of the most flavorful drip coffee that I had ever tasted!

I finished my large cup of delicious brew, and as I left with my second cup to go, I turned to Emily, and asked her if she put sugar in my coffee.  She told me, matter-of-factly, that she had not.

I thanked her, told her that I would be back soon, and that I never want sugar in my coffee at Copain.

And don’t get me started on their baked goods.  The coconut tart was heavenly, and the ham and egg scone was delicious.

Alison, another worker, introduced me to the world of croissants.  I have had croissants before, but have never really cared for them. I enjoyed this one!

Amy, the Manager at Copain, told me about how Copain began, what they are about, their offerings, and their plans to be open on Sundays, starting the last week in July.

Sunday is the only day of the week, that I get to spend the whole day with my wife, so I am so very excited about being able to share this experience with her.

Since moving to Charlotte I’ve found it difficult to find a good bakery. Until now. I’m French and grew up with the best bread and baked goods around. Nothing has been able to compare, but Copain really does come incredibly close (don’t talk to me about Amelie’s, there is nothing authentic about that place).

Copain’s chocolate croissants are flaky and airy, and their bread incredibly tasty (and difficult to eat in moderation). I also had their tomato soup which was delicious! My only suggestion would be to include more French treats and gifts in their knick knack section, it feels a little strange to see more Italian goods/items than French given they’re a French bakery! But obviously that’s a minor critique. So happy to have found Copain and looking forward to going back soon!

reviews_data.review_text: I recently visited this delightful French-style bakery, and I wasn’t disappointed. It’s bright and spacious, with indoor seating and an impressive selection including pastries, breads, salads, soups, quiches and tarts. I couldn't resist trying their chocolate croissant, and it was pure bliss. The croissant was perfectly flaky and buttery, with a chocolate drizzle across the top and a generous filling of rich chocolate for each bite. It was like a piece of Paris right in NC! The friendly staff and cozy ambiance really added to the charm of the place. I was visiting from out of town but I can assure you I’d be there on the regular if I were a local. Highly recommend.

reviews_data.review_text: My wife and I were looking for an artisanal loaf of bread, and stumbled upon Copain in our search for a delectable provisions. We found a delightful boutique establishment slightly off the beaten path, but we'll worth the trip. We found a beautiful, hefty and tasty loaf(see pictures), as well as a couple sweet treats(scone and pain chocolate). The bread was dense in the most positive way, moist, and full of flavor with a slightly sour tastebud tantalizing flavor. A little on the expensive side, but worth it for a nice treat. The cinnamon scone was pretty good as well, mild in flavor with a simple essence of cinnamon(would pair perfect with coffee). The Pain Chocolate was not my favorite, too airy without enough chocolate, but that's just my preference and would deter me from returning for other items. They also serve coffee and sell various other items, from gelato to cheese bars to take home eggplant parmesan.

reviews_data.review_text: I pre-ordered and was not sure what to expect because I had never even heard of this business before reading QC magazine. I ordered an array of food for Christmas Eve and Christmas breakfast and was pleasantly surprised how wonderful everything tasted. Highly recommend if you are having an event and don't want to be bothered with all of the cooking yourself.

The coconut cake has great flavor but was not constructed well and lacked enough icing to hold layers together.   As a tall narrow circular cake it tipped over and layers separated the minute you cut into it and made for a sloppy presentation on plates.  It's not a very big cake & at $40 I expect more.  The baguettes are the best and most authentic in town, as is the small dry salami but be prepared to pay hefty for the tiny salami.  Service when picking up items is always slow.  Either cashiers talk amongst themselves and don't notice customer standing there 2 ft away or items are not ready at pickup times even with extra 45 min cushion they were still preparing.

Barely anything selection wise to buy, not much of anything and didn’t look appealing what was just sitting left there. Management/service doesn’t seem eager to help or knowledgeable. I walked in and girl just sat chin leaning on hand/smiled kind of, barely offered help. Just sat the entire time and didn’t even say anything.
